category_full_name: CBSE > Class 11 > Physics > Mechanical Properties of Solids

questions.question: When a solid is deformed:

options.option: only the atoms or molecules at some points move from their equilibrium position.
only the atoms or molecules of the surface move from their equilibrium position.
the atoms or molecules do not move from their equilibrium position.
all the atoms or molecules are displaced from their equilibrium positions causing a change in interatomic (or intermolecular) distances.

as the ratio of change in volume ({tex}\Delta {/tex}V) to thrice the original volume V.
as the ratio of change in volume ({tex}\Delta {/tex}V) to twice the original volume V.

questions.question: According to Hooke’s law:

options.option: for small deformations, the stress and strain are inversely proportional to each other.
for small deformations, the stress and strain are proportional to each other.
for small deformations, the stress is proportional to the square of strain.
for large deformations, the stress and strain are proportional to each other.

Which of the following statements is correct?

  1. The basic reason for elasticity is interatomic force and strain energy in the material of a body is stored in the form of interatomic energy.
  2. Young’s modulus of elasticity of a body depends on the longitudinal stress applied to it.
  3. Young’s modulus of elasticity of a bo4y depends upon its size.
  4. None of the above

Which one of the following statements is wrong?

  1. Young’s modulus for a perfectly rigid body is zero.
  2. Bulk modulus is relevant for solids, liquids and gases.
  3. Rubber is less elastic than steel.
  4. The Young’s modulus and shear modulus are relevant for solids.
  5. The stretching of a coil spring is determined by its shear modulus.

  2. isothermal modulus of elasticity

Choose the wrong statement:

  1. The bulk modulus for solids is much larger than for liquids.
  2. Gases are least compressible.
  3. The reciprocal of the bulk modulus is called compressibility.
  4. For a system in equilibrium, the value of bulk modulus is always positive.

Which one of the following statement is correct? In the case of:

  1. shearing stress there is change in volume
  2. hydraulic stress there is no change in shape
  3. shearing stress there is no change in shape
  4. hydraulic stress there is no change in volume

Tensile stress at any cross-section A of the wire is IF/A
Tensile stress at any cross-section A of the wire is F/A

both balls will rise to the same height

Select the WRONG statement.

  1. Strain is fractional change produced in the body due to external forces.
  2. Strain has no dimensions.
  3. The stress is equal to the force applied per unit area.
  4. The unit of strain is N/m2.

Which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

  1. The stretching of a coil spring is determined by its shear modulus.
  2. When a deforming force is applied to a wire of steel, its length increases and radius decreases.
  3. Within elastic limit, when a deforming force is applied to steel, it shows almost perfect elastic property.
  4. The modulus of elasticity of rubber is greater than that of steel.

Mark the WRONG statement.

  1. Sliding of molecular layer is much easier than compression or expansion.
  2. Reciprocal of bulk modulus of elasticity is called compressibility.
  3. It is difficult to twist a long rod as compared to small rod.
  4. Hollow shaft is much stronger than a solid shaft of same dimensions.

A wire of an alloy gains its original dimensions back after removing the load of 200 g. This behavior gives non-linearity in the region ________ when graph of stress vs strain is plotted for the wire.

options.option: midway between proportional limit and fracture point

A uniform solid cylinder made of steel is compressed along the axis. Which of the following statements is correct?

  1. The decrease in volume of the cylinder is independent of its area of cross-section but directly proportional to its length.
  2. The decrease in volume of the cylinder is independent of its area of cross-section but inversely proportional to its length.
